description:
You are helping a localization engineer keep track of how many strings
        still need to be translated in each “.po” file of a small project.

        Project layout that already exists in the container:

        /home/user/project/i18n/
        ├── template.pot          (English template – do NOT include it in the report)
        ├── fr.po                 (French translations)
        └── es.po                 (Spanish translations)

        Goal
        ----
        1. Inside the same directory (/home/user/project/i18n/) create
           a shell script named “generate_translation_report.sh”.
        2. The script must inspect every “*.po” file in that directory
           except “template.pot”.
        3. For each language file it must count:
              • Total messages     – number of        msgid lines
                                      that are NOT the header (i.e. ignore the single
                                      msgid "" that appears at the very top of each file).
              • Translated messages – number of        msgstr lines that are NOT empty.
              • Untranslated messages – Total − Translated.
        4. The script must write the results to
           /home/user/project/i18n/translation_report.log
           using EXACTLY the format shown below so that the automated grader
           can parse it.

        Exact required format of “translation_report.log”
        -----------------------------------------------
        • First line:
              Report generated at: <ISO-8601 timestamp in UTC>
              (Use the output of date -u +'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'.)
        • Followed by a single blank line.
        • Then, for every “.po” file, a block consisting of **four** lines,
          in lexicographical order of file name (so “es” before “fr”):

              Language: <locale>            ← the locale is the filename without “.po”
              Total: <integer>
              Translated: <integer>
              Untranslated: <integer>

        • Insert one blank line between language blocks.
        • No trailing blank line at the very end of the file.

        Example (spacing, capitalisation and newlines are significant!)

            Report generated at: 2023-08-23T10:15:42Z

            Language: es
            Total: 3
            Translated: 2
            Untranslated: 1

            Language: fr
            Total: 3
            Translated: 1
            Untranslated: 2

        5. Make the script executable so it can be run with:
              ./generate_translation_report.sh

        6. When the script is executed, translation_report.log must be created
           with content that satisfies the rules above.

        Constraints / tips
        ------------------
        • Stick to standard POSIX shell utilities that are available
          in a minimal Linux installation (grep, wc, cut, etc.).
        • The task can be completed with roughly three to four commands
          inside the script (a loop, two greps, and a couple of echos).
        • Do not rely on external localisation tools (msgmerge, msgfmt, etc.).
        • Remember to ignore the very first msgid "" (the header) when counting.

        Verification
        ------------
        The automated test will:
        • Check that the script file exists and is executable.
        • Execute the script.
        • Read “translation_report.log” and verify:
              – The first line starts with “Report generated at: ” and is followed
                by a valid ISO-8601 timestamp ending with “Z”.
              – The file contains exactly two language blocks (es, fr) in that order.
              – The numeric values match the current
                contents of the .po files.
        Make sure your script produces the log exactly as specified.
truth:
Initial files provided to the agent before the task begins
        ----------------------------------------------------------

        1. /home/user/project/i18n/template.pot
        ---------------------------------------
        msgid ""
        msgstr ""
        "Project-Id-Version: Sample Project\n"
        "Pot-Creation-Date: 2022-10-10 12:00+0000\n"
        "Language: \n"

        #: src/ui.c:10
        msgid "Hello"
        msgstr ""

        #: src/ui.c:20
        msgid "Goodbye"
        msgstr ""

        #: src/ui.c:30
        msgid "Cancel"
        msgstr ""


        2. /home/user/project/i18n/fr.po
        --------------------------------
        msgid ""
        msgstr ""
        "Project-Id-Version: Sample Project\n"
        "Language: fr\n"

        #: src/ui.c:10
        msgid "Hello"
        msgstr "Bonjour"

        #: src/ui.c:20
        msgid "Goodbye"
        msgstr ""

        #: src/ui.c:30
        msgid "Cancel"
        msgstr ""


        3. /home/user/project/i18n/es.po
        --------------------------------
        msgid ""
        msgstr ""
        "Project-Id-Version: Sample Project\n"
        "Language: es\n"

        #: src/ui.c:10
        msgid "Hello"
        msgstr "Hola"

        #: src/ui.c:20
        msgid "Goodbye"
        msgstr "Adiós"

        #: src/ui.c:30
        msgid "Cancel"
        msgstr ""


        Expected numeric results that the grader will check
        ---------------------------------------------------
        • Locale es
            Total messages     = 3
            Translated         = 2
            Untranslated       = 1
        • Locale fr
            Total messages     = 3
            Translated         = 1
            Untranslated       = 2

        The grader will ignore the exact timestamp but will require that the
        rest of the file matches the following (exactly, including blank lines):

        Language: es
        Total: 3
        Translated: 2
        Untranslated: 1

        Language: fr
        Total: 3
        Translated: 1
        Untranslated: 2

        after it has stripped off the very first line beginning with
        “Report generated at: ”.

        The agent only needs write permission in /home/user/project/i18n/,
        which is already the case.
